Can't find what you're looking for? Useful links GCHQ MI5 SIS NPSA GOV.UK {"path":{"b

behavioral_patterns.value:
Router exploitation to DNS hijacking setup

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Exploit public vulnerabilities in internet-facing routers (CVE-2023-50224)
  • Step 2: Send crafted HTTP GET requests to obtain router credentials
  • Step 3: Modify DHCP/DNS settings to redirect traffic to attacker-controlled DNS servers
  • Step 4: Configure malicious DNS servers on VPS infrastructure

Detection: Monitor for unauthorized changes to router DHCP/DNS settings and unusual DNS server configurations pointing to non-standard IP ranges
behavioral_patterns.context: Router exploitation to DNS hijacking setup

behavioral_patterns.value:
DNS hijacking to credential harvesting

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Intercept DNS requests from compromised router downstream devices
  • Step 2: Selectively resolve targeted domains (email/login services) to malicious infrastructure
  • Step 3: Conduct adversary-in-the-middle attacks on redirected connections
  • Step 4: Harvest passwords, OAuth tokens, and authentication credentials

Detection: Monitor DNS resolution patterns for email/authentication domains resolving to unexpected IP addresses and detect TLS certificate anomalies

Opportunistic targeting and victim filtering

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Cast wide net by compromising multiple SOHO routers
  • Step 2: Monitor large pool of potential victims through DNS traffic
  • Step 3: Filter and triage users based on intelligence value criteria
  • Step 4: Focus exploitation efforts on high-value targets

Detection: Analyze DNS query patterns for systematic monitoring behavior and identify infrastructure serving multiple compromised networks

Multi-stage router compromise persistence

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Initial router exploitation using public vulnerabilities
  • Step 2: Set primary DNS to malicious server, secondary to original legitimate server
  • Step 3: Re-exploit same routers multiple times to update configurations
  • Step 4: Set both primary and secondary DNS to malicious servers for full control

Detection: Monitor for multiple DNS configuration changes on the same device and detect backup DNS servers pointing to suspicious infrastructure

Selective domain redirection for stealth

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Configure malicious DNS servers to monitor all incoming requests
  • Step 2: Identify requests matching targeting criteria (email/login domains)
  • Step 3: Redirect targeted domains to malicious infrastructure
  • Step 4: Pass through non-targeted domains to legitimate servers to avoid detection

Detection: Compare DNS resolution results across different DNS servers and identify selective redirection patterns for specific domain categories

VPS infrastructure setup for DNS operations

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Acquire Virtual Private Servers for malicious operations
  • Step 2: Configure VPS as DNS servers with specific banner patterns
  • Step 3: Set up SSH access on non-standard ports (56777, 35681)
  • Step 4: Deploy dnsmasq software for DNS request handling

Detection: Scan for VPS infrastructure with specific SSH port configurations and dnsmasq banners associated with malicious DNS operations
behavioral_patterns.context: VPS infrastructure setup for DNS operations

behavioral_patterns.value:
Credential theft to follow-on access

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Harvest authentication credentials through AitM attacks
  • Step 2: Collect both passwords and OAuth authentication tokens
  • Step 3: Use stolen credentials for malicious logins from different infrastructure
  • Step 4: Conduct further operations using compromised accounts

Detection: Monitor for authentication attempts using credentials shortly after DNS redirection events and detect logins from infrastructure not listed in initial compromise

Dual-cluster infrastructure operation

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Operate first cluster for broad DNS hijacking operations
  • Step 2: Set up second cluster for DNS request forwarding
  • Step 3: Forward DNS requests to remote actor-owned servers
  • Step 4: Conduct interactive operations against high-value router targets

Detection: Map DNS request forwarding patterns between different infrastructure clusters and identify interactive sessions with specific router targets

Geographic targeting with router exploitation

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Compromise MikroTik and TP-Link routers in specific regions
  • Step 2: Focus interactive operations on routers in Ukraine
  • Step 3: Identify routers of potential intelligence value
  • Step 4: Conduct targeted operations against selected high-value devices

Detection: Monitor for geographic clustering of router compromises and identify interactive sessions targeting specific regions or device types

Multi-vector router exploitation campaign

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Target multiple TP-Link router models with known vulnerabilities
  • Step 2: Exploit different router firmware versions and configurations
  • Step 3: Adapt exploitation techniques for various router architectures
  • Step 4: Scale operations across diverse router ecosystem

Detection: Monitor for exploitation attempts across multiple router vendor models and detect common attack patterns adapted for different device types

-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 20 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Domain: www.googletagmanager.com
-- ID: IOC-DOMAIN-a599aeb3
-- Generation: template (pending)
-- Description: Detects domain indicator 'www.googletagmanager.com' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Domain Detection Rule: www.googletagmanager.com
-- Source: 1220-1244
-- Type: Domain pattern match (mode: subdomain)
-- Apex Domain: www.googletagmanager.com


SELECT
    transaction_id,
    user_id,
    recipient_domain,
    sender_domain,
    transaction_url,
    created_at,
    'www.googletagmanager.com' AS matched_indicator,
    '1220-1244' AS source_span
FROM transactions
WHERE (
    recipient_domain LIKE '%www.googletagmanager.com%' OR sender_domain LIKE '%www.googletagmanager.com%' OR transaction_url LIKE '%www.googletagmanager.com%'
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 14 rules from 14 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: IOC Detection - www.googletagmanager.com
// Source: Page 1
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(90d)
| where RemoteUrl has "www.googletagmanager.com"
| summarize HitCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteUrl
| sort by HitCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: IOC Detection - advisory.It
// Source: Page 2
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(90d)
| where RemoteUrl has "advisory.It"
| summarize HitCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteUrl
| sort by HitCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: IOC Detection - office365.com
// Source: Page 3
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(90d)
| where RemoteUrl has "office365.com"
| summarize HitCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteUrl
| sort by HitCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: IOC Detection - GOV.UK
// Source: Page 5
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(90d)
| where RemoteUrl has "GOV.UK"
| summarize HitCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteUrl
| sort by HitCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - Router exploitation to DNS hijacking setup
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1557
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - DNS hijacking to credential harvesting
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1583.002
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Opportunistic targeting and victim filtering
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1583.003
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 8: Behavioral Detection - Multi-stage router compromise persistence
// Category: persistence
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1584.008
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
| where RegistryKey has "CurrentVersion\\Run"
| summarize ModCount = count(),
    UniqueKeys = dcount(RegistryKey),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName
| sort by ModCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 9: Behavioral Detection - Selective domain redirection for stealth
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1586
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 10: Behavioral Detection - VPS infrastructure setup for DNS operations
// Category: supply_chain
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1588.006
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("npm.exe", "pip.exe", "pip3.exe", "nuget.exe")
    or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("install", "update", "upgrade")
| summarize InstallCount = count(),
    UniquePackages = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where InstallCount >= 5
| sort by InstallCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 11: Behavioral Detection - Credential theft to follow-on access
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1557
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 12: Behavioral Detection - Dual-cluster infrastructure operation
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1583.002
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 13: Behavioral Detection - Geographic targeting with router exploitation
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1583.003
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 14: Behavioral Detection - Multi-vector router exploitation campaign
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1584.008
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100
compiled_outputs.splunk:
``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 14 rules from 14 indicators

``` Rule 1: IOC Detection - www.googletagmanager.com
``` Source: Page 1
index=main sourcetype=network "www.googletagmanager.com"
| stats count as hit_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, dest_ip, user
| where hit_count >= 1
| sort -hit_count
| table src_ip, dest_ip, user, hit_count, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 2: IOC Detection - advisory.It
``` Source: Page 2
index=main sourcetype=network "advisory.It"
| stats count as hit_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, dest_ip, user
| where hit_count >= 1
| sort -hit_count
| table src_ip, dest_ip, user, hit_count, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 3: IOC Detection - office365.com
``` Source: Page 3
index=main sourcetype=network "office365.com"
| stats count as hit_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, dest_ip, user
| where hit_count >= 1
| sort -hit_count
| table src_ip, dest_ip, user, hit_count, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 4: IOC Detection - GOV.UK
``` Source: Page 5
index=main sourcetype=network "GOV.UK"
| stats count as hit_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, dest_ip, user
| where hit_count >= 1
| sort -hit_count
| table src_ip, dest_ip, user, hit_count, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - Router exploitation to DNS hijacking setup
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1557
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - DNS hijacking to credential harvesting
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1583.002
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Opportunistic targeting and victim filtering
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1583.003
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 8: Behavioral Detection - Multi-stage router compromise persistence
``` Category: persistence
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1584.008
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:registry registry_path="*CurrentVersion\\Run*" action=modified
| stats count as mod_count, dc(registry_key) as unique_keys, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user, process_name
| sort -mod_count
| table dest, user, process_name, mod_count, unique_keys, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 9: Behavioral Detection - Selective domain redirection for stealth
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1586
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 10: Behavioral Detection - VPS infrastructure setup for DNS operations
``` Category: supply_chain
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1588.006
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process process_name IN ("npm.exe", "pip.exe", "pip3.exe", "nuget.exe") (process_command_line="*install*" OR process_command_line="*update*")
| stats count as install_count, dc(process_command_line) as unique_packages, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| where install_count >= 5
| sort -install_count
| table dest, user, install_count, unique_packages, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 11: Behavioral Detection - Credential theft to follow-on access
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1557
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user

| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 12: Behavioral Detection - Dual-cluster infrastructure operation
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1583.002
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user

compiled_outputs.sigma:
# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)
# Generated: 14 rules from 14 indicators
# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - www.googletagmanager.com'
id: 17190410-1d2b-5e1c-8106-1de95ca75419
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
logsource:
    category: dns
    product: any
detection:
    selection:
        dns.query.name|contains: 'www.googletagmanager.com'
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity

---
